goal: Add a side-effect-free PR/review execution contract that evaluates supplied PR metadata for a BPK release sequence and produces merge-readiness receipts without creating PRs, merging branches, calling GitHub, changing workflows, or touching runtime/frontend behavior.
scope_out: Do not create PRs, call GitHub APIs, merge branches, push to main, or change workflows.
Do not add package scripts, dependencies, environment variables, deployment notes, runtime routes, provider calls, database writes, GitHub writes, or frontend files.
Do not change BPK-011 sequencing semantics.
claims: PR/review execution readiness can be evaluated from supplied PR metadata without GitHub side effects.
All ordered sequence tasks need a PR URL, approved review, green checks, and matching head commit.
Changes-requested or pending review states block or hold execution.
The receipt is side-effect-free and tested independently.
assumptions: A caller or operator supplies PR metadata; this block does not fetch it.
Actual merge remains a later operator-controlled action.
BPK-011 sequencing is already committed and available.
dod: builder/src/bpkPrReviewExecution.ts exports side-effect-free PR review execution types and evaluator.
The evaluator returns ready, review_required, or blocked with deterministic blockers.
The evaluator detects missing PRs, commit mismatches, non-green checks, changes requested, and pending reviews.
builder/tests/bpkPrReviewExecution.test.ts covers ready, missing PR, commit mismatch, failing check, changes requested, pending review, and no-mutation behavior.
review-packets/BPK-012.md records scope, safety invariants, gates, and verification.
The focused PR review execution test and npm run typecheck in builder/ pass.
stop_conditions: A real PR creation, GitHub API call, merge, push, workflow change, deployment change, runtime change, package change, or frontend file becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: BPK-011 created a side-effect-free branch merge/release sequencing planner. BPK-012 should evaluate supplied PR/review receipts without performing GitHub actions.
